input: {"questions":[{"question":"How do you want to drive the next phase?","header":"Next phase","options":[{"label":"Answer the 8 gaps, then /plan","description":"I walk you through the 8 unanswered questions from the hackathon brief one batch at a time, then we run /plan with full context. Highest discipline, ~10-15 min of Q&A before any architecture happens."},{"label":"Sketch movement model first, then gaps","description":"Question 5 (the 'alive' feel — wandering / schooling / weather-reactive) is the demo's soul. We sketch 2-3 concrete movement models with the art kit in mind, you pick one, then we answer remaining gaps with that anchor."},{"label":"Frame demo flow first (judge POV)","description":"Question 7 (in N seconds, what does a judge get?) anchors everything else. We write the 30-second elevator pitch the visual must deliver, then back-fill the other gaps from there."},{"label":"Just /plan now, decide as we go","description":"Skip Q&A, run /plan with current context and let the discovery interview surface gaps. Faster start but PRD will have more 'TBD' until we decide later."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Which movement model anchors the observatory's 'alive' feel?","header":"Movement","options":[{"label":"Wuxing Currents (flow-field)","description":"Invisible vector field over the tsuheji map, modulated by current cosmic+IRL weather. Each puruhani drifts along the current matching its primary element; when fire weather surges, fire-affinity entities accelerate. On-chain actions emit a ripple impulse. Scales trivially to thousands; emergent schooling without per-entity AI. Reads as 'ocean of petals' — hypnotic, ambient, breath-aligned. Tradeoff: less per-character intimacy.","preview":"  WUXING CURRENTS
  ════════════════════════════════

   ╭─tsuheji-map─────────────╮
   │  ↘  ↘   ↓   ↙   ↙       │
   │ ·•·  ·•· ·•· ·•·  ·•·   │  ← entities drift
   │  ↓   ↓ ◉↘  ↓   ↘   ↓    │    along element
   │ ·•· ·•·  ·•·•·•· ·•·    │    currents
   │  ↘   ↘   ↘  ↘    ↘      │
   │  ·•·•·•·•·•·•·•·•·•·    │
   │ wood→  fire→  water→    │
   ╰─────────────────────────╯

   weather shift = field rotates
   on-chain mint  = ◉ ripple at actor
   breathing hue  = element rhythm pulse

  [O(1) per entity · 5k+ sprites trivial]"},{"label":"Element Districts (gravitational)","description":"Map loosely partitioned into 5 wuxing biomes woven into tsuheji geography. Puruhani gravitate toward their primary element's district but wander; mixed-affinity wallets are the 'interesting' drifters between zones. Weather shifts gravitational pull — rainstorm expands water district, fire entities flee. Most legible at a glance for judges. Tradeoff: feels closer to a population map than a living flow.","preview":"  ELEMENT DISTRICTS
  ════════════════════════════════

   ╭─tsuheji-map─────────────╮
   │ ╭WOOD╮     ╭─FIRE──╮    │
   │ │·•·•│     │·•·•·• │    │
   │ │ •·•│   ↗ │ •·•·• │    │  ← high-affinity
   │ ╰────╯  ↗  ╰───────╯    │    orbits tight
   │      ↗•·       ◉·•      │
   │ ╭EARTH╮  ↘ ╭─METAL─╮    │  ← drifters
   │ │·•·• │   ↘│·•·• · │    │    cross zones
   │ ╰─────╯    ╰───────╯    │
   │      ╭WATER─╮           │
   │      │·•·•·•│           │
   │      ╰──────╯           │
   ╰─────────────────────────╯

  weather shift = districts breathe larger/smaller
  judge legibility: instant — 'oh, distribution!'"},{"label":"Kanji Flock (wuxing boids)","description":"Classical flocking (separation/alignment/cohesion) but with the wuxing destructive/constructive cycle baked in: same-element entities cohere into schools, clashing pairs (water→fire, fire→metal, etc.) repel. Weather shifts rule weights. On-chain actions = an entity briefly breaks formation to animate a custom keyframe (mint = honey-burst). Most kinetic and 'alive,' best showcase of the breath-rhythm system. Tradeoff: can read as generic boids if styling isn't tight.","preview":"  KANJI FLOCK
  ════════════════════════════════

   ╭─tsuheji-map─────────────╮
   │      ·•·•·•              │  ← fire school
   │     ·•·•·•·•                 cohere
   │      ·•·•·•              │
   │                          │
   │   ╳ ← repel               │  ← water + fire
   │                          │    repel (wuxing
   │            ·•·•           │    clash)
   │           ·•·•·•          │  ← water school
   │            ·•·•           │
   │                          │
   │   ◉ ← mint event          │  ← briefly leaves
   │      ╰honey-burst         │    formation
   ╰─────────────────────────╯

  same-element  → cohere
  wuxing clash  → repel
  weather       → reweights rules
  on-chain act  → keyframe break-out"}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Which action vocabulary do we commit to for v0?","header":"Actions","options":[{"label":"Tight 3 — mint, attack, gift","description":"Smallest viable set: mint = sprite spawn / honey-burst keyframe, attack = sprite slides along inner-star (destruction) edge toward target, gift = sprite slides along pentagon (generation) edge. Three distinct visual grammars. Easiest to make each one feel polished in 4 days."},{"label":"Balanced 5 — mint, attack, gift, vote, transcend","description":"The brief's 5-action set, swapping in 'transcend' (player ascends to the center of the pentagram momentarily) for 'transfer' to give us a center-stage moment. 5 distinct keyframes — more variety, more risk that some feel less polished."},{"label":"Wuxing-pure 5 — one per element-relationship","description":"Each action is named after a wuxing relationship: feed (生), clash (克), shelter, drift, ascend. Most brand-aligned vocabulary; the actions read as cosmology rather than crypto verbs. Tradeoff: judges may need a half-second to translate."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"Where does the player perspective come from?","header":"POV","options":[{"label":"Pure observatory — no 'me'","description":"The viewer is a god. No wallet connect, no 'your puruhani.' Camera roams. The vibe is ambient and detached — you're watching a world breathe. Simplest scope; matches the brief's 'pure simulation' fork."},{"label":"Observatory + focus card","description":"Mostly god's-eye, but clicking any sprite opens the right-hand focus card (mocked wallet, mocked profile from the deterministic adapter). No connect button — anyone can inspect anyone. Adds an interactive layer without auth complexity."},{"label":"Observatory + Phantom connect","description":"Connect Phantom → your wallet's seeded mock profile becomes 'you,' camera nudges to your sprite, your actions can be triggered manually (test buttons). More demoable; opens the auth/wallet rabbit hole the brief flagged as deferred."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"This is a brownfield project with no codebase reality files. How would you like to proceed?","header":"Grounding","options":[{"label":"Skip grounding (Recommended)","description":"Proceed without /ride. Defensible here because: (a) the codebase is the freshly-scaffolded kit + 60-line hackathon brief that already captures it, (b) I explored the full surface this session, (c) hackathon clock is 4 days. Saves 5-15 min."},{"label":"Run /ride","description":"Standard codebase analysis — produces extracted-prd.md, extracted-sdd.md, component-inventory.md, consistency-report.md. Adds ~5-10 minutes; output is largely redundant with what we already have but gives clean reality citations for the PRD."},{"label":"Run /ride --enriched","description":"Full analysis with gap tracking, decision archaeology, terminology extraction. Adds ~10-15 minutes. Most thorough; overkill for a freshly-scaffolded kit."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Ready to generate the PRD (evolve /ride's draft with the resolved decisions)?","header":"Generate","options":[{"label":"Generate (Recommended)","description":"Edit grimoires/loa/prd.md: rewrite F4.7 movement model from ASSUMPTION to GROUNDED, clean §7 Open Questions (only Q-pixi remains), add Q6 coordination boundary, add v0.1→v0.4 iteration ladder, update grounding-summary counts. Conservative edits, preserves /ride's structure."},{"label":"Adjust first","description":"Tell me what to change — e.g., a decision I have wrong, a phase I should re-interview, or a section that should be larger/smaller."},{"label":"Stop here","description":"Save progress — we have a usable PRD already from /ride. Resume later with /plan or jump straight to /architect. Not what you expected? /feedback helps us fix it."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Anything to steer before architecture?","header":"Review","options":[{"label":"Continue (Recommended)","description":"Run /architect now to design the system architecture (SDD). Then /sprint-plan, then /simstim for the accelerated build cycle, then /run-bridge for iterative review — the chain you queued."},{"label":"Adjust","description":"Tell me what to change — I'll regenerate the PRD. Use this if a decision above is wrong, a missing requirement, or scope is off."},{"label":"Stop here","description":"Save progress — PRD is ready. Resume later with /architect when you want to continue. Not what you expected? /feedback helps us fix it."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"How do you want to handle the run-mode prerequisites?","header":"Setup","options":[{"label":"Set it all up + run autonomous (Recommended)","description":"Commit grimoires/ planning baseline on main, branch off to feature/observatory-v0, set run_mode.enabled: true in .loa.config.yaml, then /run sprint-1 autonomously. End state: draft PR with v0.1 idle frame implemented. Iterate from PR review + /run-bridge."},{"label":"Run sprint-1 only (smaller scope)","description":"Same setup, but invoke /run sprint-1 instead of /run sprint-plan. Single sprint, single PR. Faster than the full plan; same autonomous behavior within Sprint 1."},{"label":"Skip framework, build informally","description":"Acknowledge the framework's autonomous flow doesn't match your iterate-live desire for this hackathon. I'd violate the NEVER rule by /implement-direct. Explicit override needed — you'd be telling me to bypass review/audit gates for velocity. Risky, but legitimate hackathon call."},{"label":"Pause and rethink","description":"Stop the cycle here. PRD + SDD + sprint plan are saved. We discuss what 'iterate live' should actually look like in this framework before committing to a build approach."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"What's next?","header":"Next move","options":[{"label":"Pause — you go look at it (Recommended)","description":"Run `pnpm dev`, open /observatory, react. Iterate decisions from what you actually see. The /run loop can resume any time — commit is on feature/observatory-v0; /review-sprint + /audit-sprint + PR + /run-bridge all still available as separate calls."},{"label":"Continue the autonomous loop","description":"Run /review-sprint sprint-1 next, then /audit-sprint, then draft PR, then /run-bridge. Catches anything the implementation missed before you look. Adds 5-15 min of runtime; produces a more polished landing."},{"label":"Skip review/audit, jump to PR + /run-bridge","description":"Push the branch, open draft PR now, run /run-bridge against the open PR for adversarial review. Faster than full review/audit cycle; trades some local-loop discipline for external-eyes feedback."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"How should we ship the 5 dig-validated changes?","header":"Workflow","options":[{"label":"Ad-hoc iteration (matches recent commits) (Recommended)","description":"One logical change per commit, no sprint formalism, no review/audit gates. Matches your last 12 observatory commits. Fastest path on the 3-day hackathon clock; you watch the dev server and validate visually after each commit. Tradeoff: bypasses Loa's review+audit, which is fine for FE polish but means no programmatic correctness gate."},{"label":"Sprint-2 formal /run","description":"Register sprint-2 in the ledger with the 5 tasks, run via /run sprint-2 — implement + review-sprint + audit-sprint per task. Respects Loa CLAUDE.md to the letter. Tradeoff: heavyweight for pure FE viz polish; ~30-60% time overhead from gates that mostly check security/correctness which doesn't apply to canvas effects."},{"label":"Hybrid: ad-hoc commits, batch review at the end","description":"Iterate ad-hoc through all 5 changes, then run a single /review-sprint pass at the end before the demo recording. Catches obvious regressions without per-task overhead."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"Should I run the dev server in the background so we can validate visually after each change?","header":"Dev server","options":[{"label":"Yes, start it now (Recommended)","description":"I run pnpm dev in the background; you watch http://localhost:3000/observatory in a browser and we validate each change visually before committing."},{"label":"No, I'll start it myself","description":"You start the dev server in your own terminal — I'll just edit code and you tell me what you see."},{"label":"Skip the dev server, just edit","description":"We trust types + the existing tests and ship the changes without visual validation in this session."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"How do you want to handle the mass-conservation pulse?","header":"Pulse handling","options":[{"label":"Strip event pulses, keep weather equilibrium only (Recommended)","description":"Canvas keeps the slow weather-driven baseline (amplified element brighter, others slightly dimmer) but drops per-activity-event pulses entirely — no more ping rings, no more halo flash on each event. Cleanest separation: rail = per-event narrative; canvas = ambient world mood. The mass-conservation logic stays for the slow weather drift only."},{"label":"Keep structure, tone way down","description":"Mass-conservation logic, attention budget, and ping rings all stay — but radically attenuated. PULSE_BUMP cut to 0.3, ping rings shrunk to 60px max with 25% alpha and 1px stroke, halo response barely perceptible. The structural idea is still there if you watch for 30s."},{"label":"Revert entirely and move to change #3","description":"Roll back all the energy/ping-ring code I just added, return PentagramCanvas to its pre-change state (just weather coupling + sprite tides), and start the focus-card-on-sprite-click change next."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"How do you want to unblock ActivityRail's KpiCell?","header":"KpiCell fix","options":[{"label":"I'll synthesize a minimal KpiCell matching the FE idiom (Recommended)","description":"I add a small inline component to ActivityRail.tsx following the existing pattern (glyph + big mono value + tiny uppercase label, no chrome) so the page renders. You can refine the styling later. Fastest unblock."},{"label":"Comment out the 3 KpiCell lines + their wrapper div","description":"Most conservative — page renders without the KPI mini-cells, leaving room for you to finish the component yourself. Zero guessing on my part."},{"label":"Paste your draft KpiCell here and I'll wire it in","description":"You have a half-finished component somewhere. Drop it in chat and I'll integrate it precisely as you intend."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Which font should the actor display names use?","header":"Name font","options":[{"label":"puru-body (Inter)","description":"Sans-serif body face. Reads as natural inline name; pairs cleanly with the mono verb line."},{"label":"puru-body + font-medium","description":"Same Inter but slightly heavier for emphasis without going display-thick."},{"label":"puru-mono (Geist Mono)","description":"Match the @handle and verb line beneath. Most uniform per-row but loses hierarchy between name and handle."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"What should happen to the existing pentatonic sonification (the per-event chimes that play when activity events fire)?","header":"Sonification","options":[{"label":"Remove it entirely","description":"Music player fully replaces the sound toggle. Sonification code stays in lib/audio/sonify.ts but no longer wired in. Cleanest UX — one sound source at a time."},{"label":"Keep as separate toggle","description":"Music player + a small sonification toggle live side-by-side in the KPI strip. Two independent sound sources, user can run both."},{"label":"Bind to play state","description":"Sonification runs only while music is playing. Pressing play starts the song AND the chimes; pause stops both."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"Where should the player live?","header":"Placement","options":[{"label":"In the KPI strip (Recommended)","description":"Replace the SoundToggle slot at the right edge of the top KpiStrip. Inline with the brand chrome — matches your 'replace the icon' framing."},{"label":"Floating bottom-right HUD","description":"Like the thj-surface exhibition player. Stays out of the chrome; floats over the canvas. More room for the title to breathe."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"What should I do while you review the page?","header":"Next step","options":[{"label":"Wait for your picks","description":"Stop here. You browse /asset-test, then come back with a list of which assets to wire where (rail, KpiStrip, FocusCard, canvas)."},{"label":"Add more animations","description":"Port the heavier ChibiAgent scroll-drift + CompanionPresence blink/sleep/wake states from the source repo into the test page so you can see those too."},{"label":"Add a 'pick this' UI","description":"Bolt a checkbox + ‘copy selection as JSON’ button onto each tile so you can click through the page and hand me a structured list when done."},{"label":"Pre-stage observatory drop-in spots","description":"Survey ObservatoryClient/KpiStrip/ActivityRail/FocusCard and mark concrete slots (file:line) where each asset category would slot in cleanly. No wiring yet — just the map."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Which texture should replace the radial vignette behind the pentagram?","header":"Pentagram bg","options":[{"label":"Grain (warm)","description":"Subtle warm-amber noise. Reads as paper / parchment. Doesn't compete with sprites or vertex glyphs.","preview":"puru/patterns/grain-warm.webp

  · soft warm noise overlay
  · ~1.6 KB animated organic feel
  · blend: multiply or overlay
  · mood: parchment / ceramic"},{"label":"Cosmos (stars)","description":"Star-field pattern. More celestial, but may compete visually with the 80 sprites moving on top.","preview":"puru/patterns/cosmos-stars.webp

  · star-field scatter
  · cosmic / celestial vibe
  · risk: visually busy under sprites
  · mood: void / nightsky"},{"label":"Grain + soft tint","description":"Grain over a flat warm cloud-base — keep some warmth, add tooth. Best of both for an observatory deck feel.","preview":"grain-warm + var(--puru-cloud-base)

  · grain at 30-50% blend
  · base warm color preserved
  · no busy starfield
  · mood: warm bench/deck"}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"How should I commit the uncommitted work?","header":"Commit strategy","options":[{"label":"Two commits (Recommended)","description":"First commit: asset-test page + world-purupuru-cdn lib + PentagramCanvas CDN icons. Second commit: music player (depends on CDN lib). Clean history, each commit builds on its own."},{"label":"One bundled commit","description":"Single commit containing everything (asset-test + CDN lib + PentagramCanvas update + music player). Mixes concerns but ships all the WIP at once."},{"label":"Music player only","description":"Commit just the music player files — will fail to build since CDN lib is a dependency. Don't recommend unless you plan to commit the asset work separately right after."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"What about `permission-requests.jsonl` (analytics) and `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock`?","header":"Loa state files","options":[{"label":"Skip them (Recommended)","description":"These are local-only state files — the lock is runtime, the analytics file is your local hits. Leave them out of the commit."},{"label":"Include analytics jsonl","description":"Commit the analytics jsonl alongside (it's tracked, so git wants something with it). Skip the .lock file."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

command:
rm /tmp/world-purupuru-assets/* 2>/dev/null
rmdir /tmp/world-purupuru-assets 2>/dev/null
rm /tmp/asset-test.html /tmp/page.html 2>/dev/null
echo "scratch cleaned"
echo "---"
git log --oneline -3
input:
{"command":"rm /tmp/world-purupuru-assets/* 2>/dev/null
rmdir /tmp/world-purupuru-assets 2>/dev/null
rm /tmp/asset-test.html /tmp/page.html 2>/dev/null
echo "scratch cleaned"
echo "---"
git log --oneline -3","description":"Clean scratch files (no -rf)"}

{"questions":[{"question":"How should the bottom tab panel behave on mobile?","header":"Panel UX","options":[{"label":"Collapsible bottom sheet (Recommended)","description":"Tab bar pinned at bottom; tap a tab to slide a sheet up over the canvas (~60% of screen). Tap the same tab or backdrop to dismiss. Default = closed, so the pentagram gets full screen.","preview":"┌──────────────────┐ 
│   pentagram      │  ← canvas full-bleed
│   (full screen)  │
│                  │
│                  │
├──────────────────┤
│ ◎  ⚡  ☼          │  ← tab bar (56px)
└──────────────────┘

   tap tab ↓

┌──────────────────┐
│   pentagram      │  ← dimmed/peek
│  ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌  │
├──────────────────┤
│  ACTIVITY        │
│  • mint @kasai   │  ← sheet slides up
│  • attack @luma  │
│  • gift  @oze    │
├──────────────────┤
│ ◎ [⚡] ☼          │
└──────────────────┘"},{"label":"Always-on split","description":"Lower ~45% of the screen permanently shows the active tab's content; tabs switch which section is visible but the panel is never collapsed. No backdrop — it's a fixed split layout.","preview":"┌──────────────────┐
│  pentagram       │  ← canvas (~55%)
│                  │
├──────────────────┤
│ ◎ [⚡] ☼          │  ← tabs
├──────────────────┤
│ ACTIVITY         │
│ • mint @kasai    │  ← always visible
│ • attack @luma   │     (~45%)
│ • gift @oze      │
│ • mint @rin      │
└──────────────────┘"}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"How should the top strip (live presence / dominant element / cycle / cosmic) appear on mobile?","header":"Top strip","options":[{"label":"Move into Stats tab (Recommended)","description":"Top of the mobile screen shows just the brand wordmark + a small live pulse. The four world stats live in the Stats tab so they get full-size labels and values when the user wants them.","preview":"┌──────────────────┐
│ purupuru     ●   │  ← compact header
├──────────────────┤
│  pentagram       │
│   ...            │
├──────────────────┤
│ [◎] ⚡ ☼          │  Stats tab open ↓
├──────────────────┤
│ LIVE PRESENCE    │
│ 80               │
│ DOMINANT ELEMENT │
│ wood 24%         │
│ ...              │
└──────────────────┘"},{"label":"Keep compact KpiStrip at top","description":"The four-stat strip stays pinned at the top on mobile (same as desktop, just narrower). Stats tab then duplicates this — could feel redundant but means the headline numbers are always visible.","preview":"┌──────────────────┐
│ 80 | wood24 | … │  ← KpiStrip stays
├──────────────────┤
│  pentagram       │
│                  │
├──────────────────┤
│ [◎] ⚡ ☼          │  ← tabs
└──────────────────┘"}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"What should we do when the user denies location permission (or geolocation isn't available)?","header":"Geo fallback","options":[{"label":"IP-based fallback, then Tokyo (Recommended)","description":"Try browser geo → if denied, hit a free IP geo service for an approximate city → if that fails, fall back to Tokyo. App always shows a real-feeling location."},{"label":"Skip IP, fall back to Tokyo","description":"Browser geo only. If denied, just use Tokyo and label source as \"default\". Simpler, no extra API call."},{"label":"Show a 'allow location' nudge","description":"Block the live weather until the user grants permission. Most authentic but adds friction."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"How should light/dark mode switch?","header":"Theme switch","options":[{"label":"Auto on sunrise/sunset + manual override (Recommended)","description":"Default: light during local day, dark after sunset. Tiny toggle in the UI lets the user override (and the override persists). Storms/heavy clouds nudge darker even at noon."},{"label":"Auto only, no manual toggle","description":"Pure sunrise/sunset switch. Simplest, but the user can't escape if the auto choice clashes with their setup."},{"label":"Manual only, weather just informs accent","description":"Keep theme entirely user-controlled (or system pref); weather/time just feed visuals on the tile."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"Should real weather drive the canvas tide too, or only the WeatherTile copy?","header":"Canvas wiring","options":[{"label":"Drive canvas too (Recommended)","description":"Map UV/cloud cover → cosmic_intensity, real weather → amplifiedElement. The pentagram visibly responds to your actual sky. This is the \"feels alive\" payoff."},{"label":"Tile only, canvas keeps sine wave","description":"Smaller blast radius — only WeatherTile changes. Canvas keeps current synthetic rhythm. Faster ship, less wow."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"How should we split this session's work into commits?","header":"Commit split","options":[{"label":"Three commits by concern (Recommended)","description":"1) feat: mobile bottom-tab panel system (new MobileBottomPanel, StatsTile, ObservatoryClient wiring, ActivityRail eyebrow removal). 2) tune: pentagram assets scale proportionally to canvas radius (PentagramCanvas). 3) tune: weather tile 4-cell grid + mobile fill + eyebrow removal (WeatherTile)."},{"label":"Single bundled commit","description":"One commit covering all observatory mobile + scaling work. Faster but harder to revert any one piece independently."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"How should we handle the WeatherTile.tsx entanglement with your parallel weather work?","header":"WeatherTile","options":[{"label":"Skip WeatherTile this push (Recommended)","description":"Leave WeatherTile.tsx uncommitted entirely — you'll commit it together with your lib/weather/* changes in your other session, since the temperature_unit field reference depends on lib/weather/types.ts. Keeps concerns clean and avoids reverting your intentional change."},{"label":"Include WeatherTile + lib/weather/types.ts","description":"Bundle just the types.ts change needed so my WeatherTile compiles. Your other lib/weather changes (live.ts, index.ts) stay uncommitted. Keeps my mobile work whole at the cost of pulling one of your files into this commit."},{"label":"Revert temperature_unit reference, commit my UI changes only","description":"Temporarily set the temperature cell back to `${state.temperature_c}°` so WeatherTile compiles standalone. You'd re-add the temperature_unit suffix in your weather session. Risky — contradicts the system reminder's 'don't revert unless asked'."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"How should we split the commits?","header":"Commit split","options":[{"label":"Two commits (Recommended)","description":"A) feat(observatory): live weather + auto sunrise/sunset theme  — lib/weather/*, ObservatoryClient theme effect, WeatherTile temp unit.\nB) tune(observatory): light-mode contrast + dark variant tracks data-theme — globals.css, ActivityRail/StatsTile/WeatherTile body recess + bleed var."},{"label":"One bundled commit","description":"feat(observatory): live weather, auto theme, light-mode contrast pass — everything in a single commit. Faster, but harder to revert one piece if needed."},{"label":"Three commits","description":"Split A further: A1) live weather adapter (lib/weather/*), A2) auto theme effect, B) light mode contrast. Cleanest history but more friction for hackathon-pace work."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"What about the framework files (Loa analytics + scheduled-tasks lock)?","header":"Framework noise","options":[{"label":"Skip them (Recommended)","description":"Don't commit `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` (a lock file) or `grimoires/loa/analytics/permission-requests.jsonl` (analytics log). Leave them in your working tree; they're auto-managed and shouldn't ride with feature work."},{"label":"Add to .gitignore + skip","description":"Same as above, but also append `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` to .gitignore so future sessions don't surface it. Slight cleanup bonus."},{"label":"Bundle them in","description":"Commit them alongside the observatory work. Keeps the working tree empty post-push but mixes framework state with feature changes."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Which texture for night (when is_night=true)?","header":"Night texture","options":[{"label":"cosmos-stars (Recommended)","description":"Starfield — natural fit for the Old Horai night register; reads as the sky opening up"},{"label":"foil-warm","description":"Subtle iridescent foil pattern — more abstract, less literal-night"},{"label":"Keep grain-warm both","description":"Don't swap; only do the element-color tint + vertex aura"}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"How strong should the element-color tint on the background be?","header":"Tint strength","options":[{"label":"Hint (~8–12%) (Recommended)","description":"Barely-there bias — registers subliminally, doesn't compete with the canvas"},{"label":"Visible (~20–25%)","description":"Clearly readable as a colored room — fire days look orange, water days look blue"},{"label":"Scale by amplificationFactor","description":"Tint strength = amplificationFactor; stronger amplification → more saturated room"}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Which ambient-row framing should we use?","header":"Ambient framing","options":[{"label":"Element-led, parallel verbs","description":"Element is the subject of both rows. Natural English, parallel structure, the element name carries weight in the line. Good if you want each row to read as a small moment.","preview":"[fire png]   fire breathes today          5s ago
[user-circ]  fire emerged                 8s ago
[water png]  water holds the night        12s ago
[user-circ]  water surfaced               18s ago"},{"label":"Manifest / label (terse)","description":"Treats each row as a feed-tag. No subject, no verb — just kind · element. Reads as awareness-layer telemetry. Cleanest, lowest noise.","preview":"[fire png]   weather · fire              5s ago
[user-circ]  archetype · fire            8s ago
[water png]  weather · water             12s ago
[user-circ]  archetype · water           18s ago"},{"label":"Verb-only, icon carries element","description":"Drops element from the text entirely — the bg gradient + icon do the work. Verb is concise. Risk: if the rail scrolls fast, the element identity can blur.","preview":"[fire png]   weather shifted             5s ago
[user-circ]  archetype revealed          8s ago
[water png]  weather shifted             12s ago
[user-circ]  archetype revealed          18s ago"},{"label":"Mixed: poetic weather, label quiz","description":"Keeps weather in the gumi 'world breathes' register (matches AMBIENT_PROMPT). Simplifies quiz to a clean label. Two registers, but each fits its event well.","preview":"[fire png]   the world breathes fire     5s ago
[user-circ]  fire archetype              8s ago
[water png]  the world breathes water    12s ago
[user-circ]  water archetype             18s ago"}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Where does the indexer + storage actually live?","header":"Repo home","options":[{"label":"This repo (purupuru-ttrpg)","description":"Treat the observatory AS the score dashboard. Overkill if greenfield in this repo."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"Branch strategy?","header":"Branch","options":[{"label":"New branch off main (Recommended)","description":"Indexer is a separable concern from observatory FE polish. {"questions":[{"question":"RPC provider for the indexer (free-tier reality)?","header":"RPC provider","options":[{"label":"Helius free tier (Recommended)","description":"100k req/day, WebSocket included, devnet supported. Slightly more generous credits than Helius but more complex pricing. Requires signup."},{"label":"Triton One free tier","description":"Solana-native infra; well-regarded but sparser free-tier docs. Best of both, slight cleanup debt."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Where does observatory deploy in the separate-repo world?","header":"Observatory deploy","options":[{"label":"Vercel (Recommended)","description":"Natural fit for Next.js FE. Edge caching + image optimization + ISR. Observatory FE polls indexer service via fetch (or SSE/WS for live push). What soju's purupuru-blink already does."},{"label":"Railway alongside indexer","description":"Single platform consistency. Observatory loses Vercel benefits but ops surface is one tool. Acceptable if you already have Railway as the standard."},{"label":"Other / unsure — we'll decide later","description":"Don't lock observatory deploy now; just build the indexer as a separable service and we can deploy observatory anywhere later."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"Indexer service shape in the new repo?","header":"Indexer shape","options":[{"label":"Bare Node + Hono/Fastify (Recommended)","description":"Smallest possible service: Solana subscription + ring buffer + minimal HTTP framework (Hono is the lightest) for /events/recent + /health. ~200 LOC total. Deploys to Railway as a long-lived Node process. No Next.js overhead."},{"label":"Next.js (lighter than current Next 16 setup)","description":"Familiar territory — same instrumentation.ts pattern but in its own repo. Slight overhead for a service that doesn't need React/SSR/routing. Reuses your Next.js muscle memory."},{"label":"Bun runtime + native HTTP","description":"Smallest possible: Bun + native http. Minimal deps. Some Railway ergonomics may differ from Node. More 'modern' but adds a dimension of unfamiliarity."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Want me to survey sonar's existing handler pattern before re-planning?","header":"Sonar dig","options":[{"label":"Yes — dig into sonar src/handlers + schema (Recommended)","description":"I'll read sonar's src/handlers, src/lib, schema.graphql, and one example existing indexer (likely EVM) to understand the conventions. Most aggressive hackathon-clock move."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"Should the observatory FE consume sonar via GraphQL or REST?","header":"FE consumption","options":[{"label":"Look at sonar's existing schema.graphql first (Recommended)","description":"If sonar already exposes a GraphQL endpoint with handler-result types, observatory just adds a query for StoneClaimed and consumes it. No new API design. I'll check the schema.graphql before answering definitively."},{"label":"Add a simple REST /events/recent endpoint to sonar","description":"Lighter than GraphQL. Just a JSON GET endpoint. Observatory polls it on a 10s interval (already its pattern for weather/activity). Add to sonar even if it has GraphQL for non-realtime consumers."},{"label":"WebSocket push from sonar to observatory","description":"Realtime. Observatory subscribes via WS, sonar pushes new events as they're parsed. Most responsive but adds complexity for hackathon. Probably overkill given soju re-triggers ~10 events morning of demo."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Spin up the new Solana indexer repo — name?","header":"Repo name","options":[{"label":"mimi (Recommended)","description":"Japanese for 'ears', sister to sonar. {"questions":[{"question":"Pick a name at sonar's level of legibility?","header":"Repo name","options":[{"label":"radar (Recommended)","description":"Direct sister to sonar. sonar=sound (deep), radar=radio (air). One word, descriptive, ecosystem-coherent."},{"label":"solana-sonar","description":"Most explicit — says 'sonar's Solana counterpart' literally. Zero ambiguity for outsiders. Single word, descriptive of what's being received."}],"multiSelect":false}]}
